channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Crimson Desert Sales Have Already Hit 2m
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher Pearl Abyss has announced that its sandbox action RPG Crimson Desert, which also serves as the studio's single-player debut, has already hit a major sales milestone.

Crimson Desert launched just yesterday, but despite that, Pearl Abyss announced today that the game has passed two million sales already. The studio describes itself as "incredibly humbled" by Crimson Desert's success.

Although the game has launched to a rapturous reception (in sales terms, at least), Pearl Abyss says it's not resting on its laurels; the developer has promised to "listen closely" to community feedback and to "make improvements quickly" in order to ensure Crimson Desert is "even more enjoyable" for players.

If you're not familiar with Crimson Desert, it's an open-world action RPG developed and published by Black Desert Online studio Pearl Abyss. The game sees protagonist Kliff attempting to rebuild his broken faction after a raid by rivals kills many of his compatriots.

Crimson Desert takes place in the world of Pywel, which you can explore in its entirety. Scale is very much the name of the game here; the world is massive, and there's plenty to see and do if you're the exploration type.

Despite being a single-player release, Pearl Abyss hasn't been able to shake its MMO origins entirely for Crimson Desert; the game also contains a number of diversions and side activities, including fishing, cooking, and hunting, among many others.

Maximalism rather feels like the watchword for Crimson Desert, so if you're in the market for another one of those games that pretty much promises you can do everything within its confines, then this feels like the game for you.

Crimson Desert is available right now for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S, and if its current sales performance continues apace, this one's going to take over the world soon. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/crimson-desert-sales-2m-already
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ByteDance Sells Mobile Legends: Bang Bang Dev Moonton to Saudis for $6b
item.content:encoded:
Moonton, the studio responsible for smash hit MOBA Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, has been sold to the Saudi Arabian-owned Savvy Games Group to the tune of more than $6 billion, according to a new report.

Per news platform Nikkei (which cites "people close to the deal"), Moonton's parent company ByteDance and Savvy have signed an agreement for the latter to purchase the developer, although the deal is still subject to regulatory approval.

Moonton head Zhang Yunfan told employees today that the partnership will grant the studio "broader development opportunities" and enable it to "deliver an even better gaming experience to more players around the world", although it's not clear how the deal will affect Zhang's position as ByteDance's head of gaming.

Savvy CEO Brian Ward told Nikkei that the deal "directly supports Savvy's purpose to enable prosperity and connection through play for generations to come", with ByteDance describing the deal as "a natural next step in [Moonton's] journey".

ByteDance first acquired Moonton back in 2021, following the transaction up by acquiring a large share in Chinese studio Umi Game. However, ByteDance's heavy investment in the gaming world didn't seem to pay off; in 2022, the company laid off hundreds of its gaming employees.

Following those layoffs, it was reported in early 2024 that ByteDance was looking to get rid of its gaming studios, with possible buyers including fellow Chinese tech giant Tencent. It looks like the company has found a buyer for Moonton, at the very least.

As for what's going to happen to Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, it's unlikely to change anything significant for the game's operations, at least in the short- to medium-term.

This is the latest step in Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's efforts to invest heavily in the gaming space; the Saudi-backed Public Investment Fund, to which Savvy belongs, has invested in EA, Niantic's gaming division, and Nintendo, among many others.

Most recently, the Saudi stake in Resident Evil Requiem developer Capcom was increased to 10% after another Saudi-owned organization, the Electronic Gaming Development Company, purchased 5% of the Japanese studio's shares.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/bytedance-sells-mobile-legends-bang-bang-dev-moonton-to-saudis-for-6b
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Reigns Supreme in February 2026, Circana Says
item.content:encoded:
US sales analysis firm Circana has released its gaming sales data roundup for February, and none other than horror game Resident Evil Requiem has taken the top spot for the month, as well as for the year as a whole so far.

In a thread on Bluesky, the ever-reliable Mat Piscatella reveals that Resident Evil Requiem topped the all-format sales chart for February, as well as the year-to-date chart. It's not particularly surprising when considering the game's exemplary sales performance since launch.

Second place in February goes to NBA 2K26, a perennially popular entry, although it's a bit of an "always the bridesmaid, never the bride" situation; the game also found itself in second place last month, having been beaten out by Call of Duty: Black Ops 7.

Activision's latest first-person shooter finds itself in third place for February, with Minecraft in fourth and Sony's bug shooter Helldivers 2 (yes, alright, you also shoot other things) in fifth.

EA takes the next three spots with EA Sports FC 26, Battlefield 6, and Madden NFL 26 respectively, and the top ten is rounded out by Dragon Quest VII: Reimagined in ninth place and Grand Theft Auto 5 in tenth.

Many of the same offenders can be found in the year-to-date chart, although Dragon Quest is nowhere to be seen and Red Dead Redemption 2 is present in ninth place.

In hardware terms, the PlayStation 5 was February's best-selling console, with the Nintendo Switch 2 taking second place in both unit and dollar terms. The hardware market overall rose by 22%, Piscatella and Circana say.

As ever, I'd recommend checking out Piscatella's full thread if you want to see some more nerdy stats about the state of the gaming industry as a whole last month. Stay tuned for March's results as soon as Circana and Piscatella release them!

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/us-gaming-sales-data-february-2026-resident-evil-requiem
